definitions: To start suddenly, often with violence or intensity.
To escape from a place or situation, especially by force.
To develop a skin rash or other physical manifestation.
To become public or widely known.
examples: War broke out in the region.
The prisoner broke out of jail.
She broke out in a rash.
The scandal broke out last week.
pastTenses: broke out (past simple), broken out (past perfect)
